section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: She uses a smart device to control her home appliances.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: This gadget helps you track your fitness.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Automation has reduced the need for manual labor.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: We live in the digital age, where everything is online—shopping, learning, even friendships. This makes people spend more time on screens and less time being active.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Modern society relies heavily on technology.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: The convenience of using apps to order food or call a taxi means people don’t walk or cook as much as before.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Machines and apps do things with great efficiency, like cleaning or organizing, but they also reduce the need for human effort.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Some apps are designed to boost productivity, like planners or timers, but people often use their phones for games instead, which lowers their work output.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: A tablet is a small computer. Many students use tablets to watch videos or play games for hours, which stops them from doing homework or exercising.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: To binge-watch means to watch many episodes in one sitting. Some teenagers binge-watch dramas all weekend and forget to study or sleep.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: People scroll through social media by moving their finger up and down the screen. They often do this for hours without realizing how much time they’ve wasted.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Virtual reality creates a fake world using technology. It’s fun, but it can make people ignore real-life activities like sports or socializing.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Digital consumption means using digital content like videos, games, or social media. Too much digital consumption can lead to laziness and poor health.

section: 5.1 Vocabulary Related to Technology in General
sentence: Remote access lets people work or study from home using the internet. It’s helpful, but it also means people move around less.
She became inactive after quitting sports.
To procrastinate means to delay doing something. Many students procrastinate by watching YouTube instead of doing homework.
A sedentary lifestyle means sitting most of the time. Using phones and computers too much can lead to this unhealthy way of living.
Dependence means needing something too much. Some people have a strong dependence on their phones and feel lost without them.
Complacency means being too comfortable and not trying to improve. When technology does everything for us, we become complacent and stop learning new skills.
Lack of motivation means not feeling like doing anything. Technology can cause this when people get used to easy entertainment and avoid hard work.
Students who are too passive in class may miss important learning opportunities.
To facilitate means to make something easier. Technology can facilitate learning, but only if students use it for studying, not for games.
To simplify means to make something less complicated. Apps simplify tasks like shopping or cooking, but they also make people less independent.
To enhance means to improve something. Technology can enhance communication, but it may reduce real-life conversations.
Technology can improve quality of life by making things easier, like helping disabled people. But for others, it can lead to laziness and poor health.
Some tools increase productivity by helping people work faster. But if used wrongly, they can distract people and reduce their focus.
Technology can empower students by giving them access to online learning, but some use it only for entertainment.
Smart homes automate tasks like turning on lights or cleaning floors, so people don’t need to do them manually anymore.
Online banking is a time-saving solution for busy individuals.
Apps help people organize their schedules, but many still forget tasks because they rely too much on reminders.
Machines can increase output in factories, but workers may become less skilled over time.
Real-time collaboration tools like Google Docs let people work together instantly, but they also reduce face-to-face teamwork.
Educational apps encourage learning, but students often choose games over study tools.
Technology allows active engagement in tasks and goals, but some users ignore these features.
Over-reliance on GPS means people can’t find places without their phones anymore.
Phone addiction makes it hard for students to focus in class or sleep at night.
Social media is a big distraction during study time, making it hard to concentrate.
Using technology too much can decrease physical activity, as people sit for long hours.
Texting can reduce social interaction because people stop talking face-to-face.
Screen time causes health problems like eye strain.
The overuse of video apps leads to poor sleep and less time for homework.
People with poor self-discipline often find it hard to resist distractions from their phones.
Technology gives instant gratification—like fast likes or replies—but it makes people impatient in real life.
Copying answers from the internet leads to reduced critical thinking skills.
Notifications cause distracted attention, so students can’t focus for long periods.
Because apps do everything for them, some students experience a loss of self-motivation and stop trying on their own.

section: 6.2 Effects on People and Safety, Actions and Solutions, and Environmental Issues
sentence: We must take action to reduce plastic waste by bringing our own containers.

sentence: The school launched a campaign to reduce food waste after noticing how much lunch was thrown away.

sentence: Fast fashion creates textile waste, so I started buying fewer clothes and choosing second-hand items.

sentence: We replaced old fans with energy-efficient appliances to lower our classroom’s electricity use.

section: 6.2 Effects on People and Safety, Actions and Solutions, and Environmental Issues
sentence: You should bring your own container to the tuck shop to help reduce plastic waste.

section: 6.2 Effects on People and Safety, Actions and Solutions, and Environmental Issues
sentence: To protect the environment, we should reduce waste, reuse materials, recycle properly, and replace harmful products with eco-friendly ones.
Name-calling based on race or appearance should never be tolerated in school.
A cultural misunderstanding happened when students laughed at a classmate’s traditional festival outfit.
Some students tease others because of their clothes or accent, which makes them feel hurt.
I felt left out when my classmates didn’t invite me to their group project.
We should respect classmates who have different backgrounds or abilities.
We should be kind to others, especially when they are new or shy.
We must educate students about the harm caused by name-calling and teach them to respect differences.
Our class started a campaign to stop teasing and promote kindness towards classmates from different backgrounds.
Exam pressure made me feel anxious, so I started using a study planner to manage my time.
After experiencing sleepless nights, I struggled to concentrate in class and noticed a drop in school performance.
Due to peer pressure, I joined a social media challenge that I didn’t feel comfortable with
Relationship problems can occur when students feel stressed or anxious and begin to argue with friends
The fear of failure stopped me from joining the debate team, even though I was interested.
I experienced burnout after revising for hours every night without taking breaks.
Peer comparison on social media made me feel like I wasn’t good enough.
Journaling helped me express my feelings when I felt overwhelmed by schoolwork.
I started to practise mindfulness before exams to help me stay calm and focused.
I stopped drinking sugary drinks after learning how they contribute to obesity.
I realised that eating too much processed food like instant noodles and sausages made me feel tired during lessons.
I reduced my portion size by using a smaller lunchbox and felt more energetic in the afternoon.
Physical inactivity became a problem when I spent all weekend gaming instead of going outside.
A sedentary lifestyle, like sitting all day playing video games, can lead to weight gain.
I eat a balanced diet with plenty of vegetables to stay healthy.
I might gain weight if I stop playing basketball and eat junk food.
I decided to exercise regularly by jogging every evening with my cousin.
The school promotes waste reduction by encouraging students to use fewer paper handouts.
We should use eco-friendly bags instead of single-use plastic ones.
Walking or cycling to school helps reduce your personal carbon footprint.
Marine pollution is a serious problem that harms fish and sea turtles.
Fish often eat microplastics by mistake, which can eventually enter the human food chain.
Wildlife conservation is important to protect endangered animals from extinction.
We must take action immediately to stop climate change.
Try to avoid buying products with excessive packaging to save the environment.
You can replace disposable bottles with a durable, reusable water bottle.
Don't throw away paper; put it in the recycling bin instead.
Chemicals released into the river can harm the environment significantly.
Single-use plastic forks are wasteful because they are thrown away after one meal.
I refused a plastic straw to reduce waste.
Please say "no" to disposable cutlery when you order takeaway food.
This toy comes with too much unnecessary plastic packaging.
Most plastic waste ends up in a landfill and stays there for hundreds of years.
Using biodegradable cutlery is a better choice because it breaks down naturally.
Bring your own food containers to the canteen to avoid using foam boxes.
Students should separate their waste and put it into the correct recycling bins.
The school upgraded its campus facilities to include more water dispensers.
I try to use reusable lunchbox containers every day.
The Environmental Club will put up posters to remind students to recycle.
The principal will make morning announcements about the new "No Plastic" policy.
We need to reduce consumption of electricity to save money and the planet.
It is easy to switch to reusable alternatives like metal straws or cloth bags.
Remember to bring your own bottle (BYOB) for the school picnic.
We should try to minimise waste by buying only what we need.
Choose loose fruit instead of pre-packed ones to avoid unnecessary packaging.
It takes time to adopt sustainable habits, but it is worth the effort.
The cafeteria plans to introduce biodegradable materials for all takeaway boxes.
The school will install water dispensers on every floor to encourage students to drink water.
The Student Union will organise no-plastic days once a month to raise awareness.
It is important to sort rubbish properly into paper, plastic, and metal bins.

section: 7.5 Benefits to students and the environment
meaning: 推廣更健康的生活方式
sentence: Eating more vegetables and less fast food helps promote a healthier lifestyle.
The club plans to implement a campaign to save water next week.
The government will launch an initiative to reduce plastic bag use next month.
We made a video to raise awareness about the dangers of climate change.
Teachers promote green habits by showing students how to recycle correctly.
We offered prizes to encourage participation in the recycling slogan contest.
The speaker shared practical strategies for reducing household waste.
Banning plastic straws is one of the effective measures to help the ocean.
Cleaning the beach was a collaborative effort by students, teachers, and parents.
Global warming is an urgent issue that requires immediate attention from everyone.
Pollution has long-lasting consequences on the health of future generations.
Burning plastic releases harmful substances into the air.
We must protect the environment today to ensure a sustainable future for our children.
